title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhed: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

Former Spanish PM Mariano Rajoy makes racist remarks about France's football team
subhed: In an op-ed published after Spain qualified for the World Cup semifinals, Mariano Rajoy, former leader of the right-wing People's Party, said there were 'no French' in France's squad.

title: The rush to air-conditioned spaces: 'Full house at the cinema for 2 hours and 40 minutes of AC'
subhed: Guillemette Faure explores the growing trend in France of "AC surfing," as city dwellers move from one air-conditioned public space to another.

title: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
subhed: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

title: Before Emile Zola, this forgotten writer was the first to defend Alfred Dreyfus
subhed: The journalist Bernard Lazare fought relentlessly to defend Captain Dreyfus and denounce the antisemitism that drove his conviction. On Sunday, France will mark its first national day commemorating the rehabilitation of the Jewish officer.

subhed: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

title: After a year eyeing France's presidency, Bardella has to settle for deputy
subhed: On Marine Le Pen's first campaign trip after being cleared to run for president despite her conviction, her would-be stand-in Jordan Bardella looked stern at her side.
